name: Belgium

authority.name: FPS Economy — Federal Public Service Economy, S.M.E.s, Self-employed and Energy (Federale Overheidsdienst Economie, K.M.O., Middenstand en Energie / SPF Économie, P.M.E., Classes moyennes et Énergie), Belgian eIDAS supervisory body and national Trusted List operator
authority.operator: Accredited QTSPs under eIDAS (Belgian Mobile ID / itsme, Zetes / ZetesConfidens, GMO GlobalSign nv/sa, DigiCert QuoVadis Belgium, Certipost / Belgium Root CA (eID), Banqup, Docbyte, and others)

governingLaw: Reglamento eIDAS (UE) 910/2014 · Livre XII du Code de droit économique (Digital Economy) · Loi du 21 juillet 2016 (mise en œuvre eIDAS)

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the Belgian Trusted List (tsl-be-v6.xml, FPS Economy)

relatedLinks.label: EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: Belgium's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (Belgian Mobile ID / itsme, Zetes and ZetesConfidens, GMO GlobalSign nv/sa, DigiCert QuoVadis Belgium, Certipost's Belgium Root CA hierarchy behind the Belgian eID, Banqup, and Docbyte, among others) supervised by the FPS Economy — the Federal Public Service Economy, S.M.E.s, Self-employed and Energy — and published in the Belgian national eIDAS Trusted List (tsl-be-v6.xml, ETSI TS 119 612). The 52 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Belgian TSL's signature (signer: C=BE, ST=Brussels Hoofdstedelijk Gewest, L=Sint-Joost-ten-Node, O=Federale Overheidsdienst Economie\, K.M.O\, Middenstand en Energie, CN=Federale Overheidsdienst Economie\, K.M.O\, Middenstand en Energie, 2.5.4.97=NTRBE-0314.595.348; cert SHA-256 fbec3a3b1bb26cd02e7737b9e3ea289caa7d45adf608265122ea0db3d1de66ce) against the identity the LOTL declares for Belgium, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Key algorithms across the set are mixed: 31 CAs use RSA-4096, 17 use EC P-384, 3 use EC P-521, and 1 uses EC P-256. Two certificates in the set (QuoVadis Belgium Issuing CA G2 and ZETES TSP QUALIFIED CA 001) have passed their notAfter and are retained as archived-but-granted entries; they surface under the Expired status filter.
